constructors.name: SubD()
constructors.summary: Create a new instance of SubD geometry

params.name: info
params.doc: [Missing <param name="info"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.#ctor(System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo,System.Runtime.Serialization.StreamingContext)"]

params.name: context
params.doc: [Missing <param name="context"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.#ctor(System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo,System.Runtime.Serialization.StreamingContext)"]

methods.name: ClearEvaluationCache
methods.summary: Clear all cached evaluation information (meshes, surface points, bounding boxes, ...) that depends on edge tags, vertex tags, and the location of vertex control points.

methods.name: ComponentFromComponentIndex
methods.summary: Returns a SubDComponent, either a SubDEdge, SubDFace, or SubDVertex, from a component index.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: The component index.

returns.doc: The SubDComponent if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: ComponentIndex
methods.summary: If this piece of geometry is a component in something larger, like a BrepEdge in a Brep, then this function returns the component index.

returns.doc: This object's component index. If this object is not a sub-piece of a larger geometric entity, then the returned index has m_type = ComponentIndex.InvalidType and m_index = -1.

methods.name: ConstructConstObject
methods.signature: protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)
methods.summary: Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

params.name: parentObject
params.doc: The parent object.

params.name: subobjectIndex
params.doc: The sub-object index.

methods.name: CopyEvaluationCache
methods.summary: Expert function that copies cached evaluations of component subdivision points and limit surface information from src to this. Typically this is done for performance critical situations like control point editing: - Copy a SubD to be modified (this does not copy the evaluation cache) - Copy the evaluation cache from the unmodified SubD - Modify the SubD copy - Update the surface mesh cache so that only the modified parts are recalculated - Display, meshing, bounding boxes on the modified SubD are now available

params.name: src
params.doc: The SubD from which to copy cached evaluations

returns.doc: True if the cache was fully copied, false otherwise

methods.name: CreateFromCylinder
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateFromCylinder(Cylinder cylinder, uint circumferenceFaceCount, uint heightFaceCount, SubDEndCapStyle endCapStyle, SubDEdgeTag endCapEdgeTag, SubDComponentLocation radiusLocation)
methods.summary: Creates a SubD form of a cylinder.

params.name: cylinder
params.doc: The defining cylinder.

params.name: circumferenceFaceCount
params.doc: Number of faces around the cylinder.

params.name: heightFaceCount
params.doc: Number of faces in the top-to-bottom direction.

params.name: endCapStyle
params.doc: The end cap style.

params.name: endCapEdgeTag
params.doc: The end cap edge tag.

params.name: radiusLocation
params.doc: The SubD component location.

returns.doc: A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: CreateFromLoft
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateFromLoft(IEnumerable<NurbsCurve> curves, bool closed, bool addCorners, bool addCreases, int divisions)
methods.summary: Creates a SubD lofted through shape curves.
methods.remarks: Shape curves must be in the proper order and orientation and have point counts for the desired surface. Shape curves must be either all open or all closed.

params.name: curves
params.doc: An enumeration of SubD-friendly NURBS curves to loft through.

params.name: closed
params.doc: Creates a SubD that is closed in the lofting direction. Must have three or more shape curves.

params.name: addCorners
params.doc: With open curves, adds creased vertices to the SubD at both ends of the first and last curves.

params.name: addCreases
params.doc: With kinked curves, adds creased edges to the SubD along the kinks.

params.name: divisions
params.doc: The segment number between adjacent input curves.

returns.doc: A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: CreateFromMesh(Mesh)
methods.summary: Create a new SubD from a mesh.

params.name: mesh
params.doc: The input mesh.

returns.doc: A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: CreateFromMesh(Mesh, SubDCreationOptions)
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateFromMesh(Mesh mesh, SubDCreationOptions options)
methods.summary: Create a new SubD from a mesh.

params.name: mesh
params.doc: The input mesh.

params.name: options
params.doc: The SubD creation options.

returns.doc: A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: CreateFromSurface
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateFromSurface(Surface surface, SubDFromSurfaceMethods method, bool corners)
methods.summary: Create a SubD that approximates the surface. If the surface is a SubD friendly NURBS surface and withCorners is true, then the SubD and input surface will have the same geometry.

params.name: surface
params.doc: [Missing <param name="surface"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.CreateFromSurface(Rhino.Geometry.Surface,Rhino.Geometry.SubDFromSurfaceMethods,System.Boolean)"]

params.name: method
params.doc: Selects the method used to calculate the SubD.

params.name: corners
params.doc: If the surface is open, then the corner vertices with be tagged as VertexTagCorner. This makes the resulting SubD have sharp corners to match the appearance of the input surface.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.CreateFromSurface(Rhino.Geometry.Surface,Rhino.Geometry.SubDFromSurfaceMethods,System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: CreateFromSweep(NurbsCurve, NurbsCurve, IEnumerable<NurbsCurve>, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateFromSweep(NurbsCurve rail1, NurbsCurve rail2, IEnumerable<NurbsCurve> shapes, bool closed, bool addCorners)
methods.summary: Fits a SubD through a series of profile curves that define the SubD cross-sections and two curves that defines SubD edges.
methods.remarks: Shape curves must be in the proper order and orientation. Shape curves must have the same point counts and rail curves must have the same point counts. Shape curves will relocated to the nearest pair of Greville points on the rails. Shape curves will be made at each pair of rail edit points where there isn't an input shape.

params.name: rail1
params.doc: The first SubD-friendly NURBS curve to sweep along.

params.name: rail2
params.doc: The second SubD-friendly NURBS curve to sweep along.

params.name: shapes
params.doc: An enumeration of SubD-friendly NURBS curves to sweep through.

params.name: closed
params.doc: Creates a SubD that is closed in the rail curve direction.

params.name: addCorners
params.doc: With open curves, adds creased vertices to the SubD at both ends of the first and last curves.

returns.doc: A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: CreateFromSweep(NurbsCurve, IEnumerable<NurbsCurve>, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Vector3d)
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateFromSweep(NurbsCurve rail1, IEnumerable<NurbsCurve> shapes, bool closed, bool addCorners, bool roadlikeFrame, Vector3d roadlikeNormal)
methods.summary: Fits a SubD through a series of profile curves that define the SubD cross-sections and one curve that defines a SubD edge.
methods.remarks: Shape curves must be in the proper order and orientation. Shape curves must have the same point counts and rail curves must have the same point counts. Shape curves will relocated to the nearest pair of Greville points on the rails. Shape curves will be made at each pair of rail edit points where there isn't an input shape.

params.name: rail1
params.doc: A SubD-friendly NURBS curve to sweep along.

params.name: shapes
params.doc: An enumeration of SubD-friendly NURBS curves to sweep through.

params.name: closed
params.doc: Creates a SubD that is closed in the rail curve direction.

params.name: addCorners
params.doc: With open curves, adds creased vertices to the SubD at both ends of the first and last curves.

params.name: roadlikeFrame
params.doc: Determines how sweep frame rotations are calculated. If false (Freeform), frame are propagated based on a reference direction taken from the rail curve curvature direction. If true (Roadlike), frame rotations are calculated based on a vector supplied in "roadlikeNormal" and the world coordinate system.

params.name: roadlikeNormal
params.doc: If roadlikeFrame = true, provide 3D vector used to calculate the frame rotations for sweep shapes. If roadlikeFrame = false, then pass Unset.

returns.doc: A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: CreateGlobeSphere
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateGlobeSphere(Sphere sphere, SubDComponentLocation vertexLocation, uint axialFaceCount, uint equatorialFaceCount)
methods.summary: Creates a SubD sphere made from polar triangle fans and bands of quads. The result resembles a globe with triangle fans at the poles and the edges forming latitude parallels and longitude meridians.

params.name: sphere
params.doc: Location, size and orientation of the sphere.

params.name: vertexLocation
params.doc: If vertexLocation = SubDComponentLocation::ControlNet, then the control net points will be on the surface of the sphere. Otherwise the limit surface points will be on the sphere.

params.name: axialFaceCount
params.doc: Number of faces along the sphere's meridians. (axialFaceCount >= 2) For example, if you wanted each face to span 30 degrees of latitude, you would pass 6 (=180 degrees/30 degrees) for axialFaceCount.

params.name: equatorialFaceCount
params.doc: Number of faces around the sphere's parallels. (equatorialFaceCount >= 3) For example, if you wanted each face to span 30 degrees of longitude, you would pass 12 (=360 degrees/30 degrees) for equatorialFaceCount.

returns.doc: If the input parameters are valid, a SubD globe sphere is returned. Otherwise null is returned.

methods.name: CreateIcosahedron
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateIcosahedron(Sphere sphere, SubDComponentLocation vertexLocation)
methods.summary: Creates a SubD sphere based on an icosohedron (20 triangular faces and 5 valent vertices). This is a goofy topology for a Catmull-Clark subdivision surface (all triangles, all vertices have 5 edges). You may want to consider using the much behaved result from CreateSubDQuadSphere(sphere, vertexLocation, 1) or even the result from CreateSubDGlobeSphere().

params.name: sphere
params.doc: Location, size and orientation of the sphere.

params.name: vertexLocation
params.doc: If vertexLocation = SubDComponentLocation::ControlNet, then the control net points will be on the surface of the sphere. Otherwise the limit surface points will be on the sphere.

returns.doc: If the input parameters are valid, a SubD icosahedron is returned. Otherwise null is returned.

methods.name: CreateQuadSphere
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateQuadSphere(Sphere sphere, SubDComponentLocation vertexLocation, uint quadSubdivisionLevel)
methods.summary: Creates a SubD sphere made from quad faces.

params.name: sphere
params.doc: Location, size and orientation of the sphere.

params.name: vertexLocation
params.doc: If vertexLocation = SubDComponentLocation::ControlNet, then the control net points will be on the surface of the sphere. Otherwise the limit surface points will be on the sphere.

params.name: quadSubdivisionLevel
params.doc: The resulting sphere will have 6*4^subdivision level quads. (0 for 6 quads, 1 for 24 quads, 2 for 96 quads, ...).

returns.doc: If the input parameters are valid, a SubD quad sphere is returned. Otherwise null is returned.

methods.name: CreateTriSphere
methods.signature: public static SubD CreateTriSphere(Sphere sphere, SubDComponentLocation vertexLocation, uint triSubdivisionLevel)
methods.summary: Creates a SubD sphere made from triangular faces. This is a goofy topology for a Catmull-Clark subdivision surface (all triangles and all vertices have 5 or 6 edges). You may want to consider using the much behaved result from CreateSubDQuadSphere() or even the result from CreateSubDGlobeSphere().

params.name: sphere
params.doc: Location, size and orientation of the sphere.

params.name: vertexLocation
params.doc: If vertexLocation = SubDComponentLocation::ControlNet, then the control net points will be on the surface of the sphere. Otherwise the limit surface points will be on the sphere.

params.name: triSubdivisionLevel
params.doc: The resulting sphere will have 20*4^subdivision level triangles. (0 for 20 triangles, 1 for 80 triangles, 2 for 320 triangles, ...).

returns.doc: If the input parameters are valid, a SubD tri sphere is returned. Otherwise null is returned.

methods.name: DataCRC
methods.summary: Returns a CRC calculated from the information that defines the object. This CRC can be used as a quick way to see if two objects are not identical.

params.name: currentRemainder
params.doc: The current remainder value.

returns.doc: CRC of the information the defines the object.

methods.name: DeleteAllUserStrings
methods.summary: (Inherited from GeometryBase.)

methods.name: DeleteUserString
methods.summary: (Inherited from GeometryBase.)

params.name: key
params.doc: [Missing <param name="key"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase.DeleteUserString(System.String)"]

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.GeometryBase.DeleteUserString(System.String)"]

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: Deletes the underlying native pointer during a Dispose call or GC collection

params.name: disposing
params.doc: True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

methods.name: Duplicate
methods.summary: Constructs a deep (full) copy of this object.

returns.doc: An object of the same type as this, with the same properties and behavior.

methods.name: DuplicateEdgeCurves()
methods.summary: Gets Nurbs form of all edges in this SubD, with clamped knots. NB: Does not update the SubD evaluation cache before getting the edges.

returns.doc: An array of edge curves.

methods.name: DuplicateEdgeCurves(Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public Curve[] DuplicateEdgeCurves(bool boundaryOnly, bool interiorOnly, bool smoothOnly, bool sharpOnly, bool creaseOnly, bool clampEnds)
methods.summary: Gets Nurbs form of edges in this SubD. NB: Does not update the SubD evaluation cache before getting the edges.

params.name: boundaryOnly
params.doc: If true, then only the boundary edges are duplicated. If false, then all edges are duplicated. If both boundaryOnly and interiorOnly are true, an empty array is returned.

params.name: interiorOnly
params.doc: If true, then only the interior edges are duplicated. If false, then all edges are duplicated. Note: interior edges with faces of different orientations are also returned. If both boundaryOnly and interiorOnly are true, an empty array is returned.

params.name: smoothOnly
params.doc: If true, then only the smooth (and not sharp) edges are duplicated. If false, then all edges are duplicated. If both smoothOnly and sharpOnly and creaseOnly are true, an empty array is returned.

params.name: sharpOnly
params.doc: If true, then only the sharp edges are duplicated. If false, then all edges are duplicated. If both smoothOnly and sharpOnly and creaseOnly are true, an empty array is returned.

params.name: creaseOnly
params.doc: If true, then only the creased edges are duplicated. If false, then all edges are duplicated. If both smoothOnly and sharpOnly and creaseOnly are true, an empty array is returned.

params.name: clampEnds
params.doc: If true, the end knots are clamped. Otherwise the end knots are(-2,-1,0,...., k1, k1+1, k1+2).

returns.doc: Array of edge curves on success.

methods.name: DuplicateShallow
methods.summary: Constructs a light copy of this object. By "light", it is meant that the same underlying data is used until something is done to attempt to change it. For example, you could have a shallow copy of a very heavy mesh object and the same underlying data will be used when doing things like inspecting the number of faces on the mesh. If you modify the location of one of the mesh vertices, the shallow copy will create a full duplicate of the underlying mesh data and the shallow copy will become a deep copy.

returns.doc: An object of the same type as this object. This behavior is overridden by implementing classes.

methods.name: EnsurePrivateCopy
methods.summary: If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: Flip
methods.summary: Reverses the orientation of all SubD normals.

returns.doc: True if successful.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Boolean)
methods.summary: Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the geometry.

params.name: accurate
params.doc: If true, a physically accurate bounding box will be computed. If not, a bounding box estimate will be computed. For some geometry types there is no difference between the estimate and the accurate bounding box. Estimated bounding boxes can be computed much (much) faster than accurate (or "tight") bounding boxes. Estimated bounding boxes are always similar to or larger than accurate bounding boxes.

returns.doc: The bounding box of the geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Plane)
methods.summary: Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

params.name: plane
params.doc: Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

returns.doc: A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Transform)
methods.summary: Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the world axis aligned bounding box for the transformed geometry.

params.name: xform
params.doc: Transformation to apply to object prior to the BoundingBox computation. The geometry itself is not modified.

returns.doc: The accurate bounding box of the transformed geometry in world coordinates or BoundingBox.Empty if not bounding box could be found.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox(Plane, Box)
methods.signature: public BoundingBox GetBoundingBox(Plane plane, out Box worldBox)
methods.summary: Aligned Bounding box solver. Gets the plane aligned bounding box.

params.name: plane
params.doc: Orientation plane for BoundingBox.

params.name: worldBox
params.doc: Aligned box in World coordinates.

returns.doc: A BoundingBox in plane coordinates.

methods.name: GetObjectData
methods.signature: public virtual void GetObjectData(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)
methods.summary: Populates a System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo with the data needed to serialize the target object.

params.name: info
params.doc: The System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo to populate with data.

params.name: context
params.doc: The destination (see System.Runtime.Serialization.StreamingContext) for this serialization.

methods.name: GetUserString
methods.summary: Gets user string from this geometry.

params.name: key
params.doc: id used to retrieve the string.

returns.doc: string associated with the key if successful. null if no key was found.

methods.name: GetUserStrings
methods.summary: Gets a copy of all (user key string, user value string) pairs attached to this geometry.

returns.doc: A new collection.

methods.name: InterpolateSurfacePoints(Point3d[])
methods.summary: Modifies the SubD so that the SubD vertex limit surface points are equal to surface_points[]

params.name: surfacePoints
params.doc: Points for limit surface to interpolate. surface_points[i] is the location for the i-th vertex returned by SubVertexIterator vit(this)

returns.doc: True on success

methods.name: InterpolateSurfacePoints(UInt32[], Point3d[])
methods.signature: public bool InterpolateSurfacePoints(uint[] vertexIndices, Point3d[] surfacePoints)
methods.summary: Modifies the SubD so that the SubD vertex limit surface points of the listed vertices are equal to surface_points[].

params.name: vertexIndices
params.doc: Ids of the vertices to interpolate. Other vertices remain fixed.

params.name: surfacePoints
params.doc: Points for limit surface to interpolate. surface_points[i] is the location for the vertex returned by this.Vertices.Find(vertexIndices[i]).

returns.doc: True on success

methods.name: IsValidWithLog
methods.summary: Determines if an object is valid. Also provides a report on errors if this object happens not to be valid.

params.name: log
params.doc: A textual log. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if this object is valid; false otherwise.

methods.name: JoinSubDs(IEnumerable<SubD>, Double, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static SubD[] JoinSubDs(IEnumerable<SubD> subdsToJoin, double tolerance, bool joinedEdgesAreCreases)
methods.summary: Joins an enumeration of SubDs to form as few as possible resulting SubDs. There may be more than one SubD in the result array.
methods.remarks: NOTE: All of the input SubDs are copied and added to the result array in one form or another. NOTE: Symmetry information is removed from newly joined SubDs.See also comments in SubD.JoinSubDs with the bPreserveSymmetry parameter.

params.name: subdsToJoin
params.doc: An enumeration of SubDs to join.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: The join tolerance.

params.name: joinedEdgesAreCreases
params.doc: If true, merged boundary edges will be creases. If false, merged boundary edges will be smooth.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.JoinSubDs(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Rhino.Geometry.SubD},System.Double,System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: JoinSubDs(IEnumerable<SubD>, Double, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public static SubD[] JoinSubDs(IEnumerable<SubD> subdsToJoin, double tolerance, bool joinedEdgesAreCreases, bool preserveSymmetry)
methods.summary: Joins an enumeration of SubDs to form as few as possible resulting SubDs. There may be more than one SubD in the result array.
methods.remarks: NOTE: All of the input SubDs are copied and added to the result array in one form or another.

params.name: subdsToJoin
params.doc: An enumeration of SubDs to join.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: The join tolerance.

params.name: joinedEdgesAreCreases
params.doc: If true, merged boundary edges will be creases. If false, merged boundary edges will be smooth.

params.name: preserveSymmetry
params.doc: If true, and if all inputs share the same symmetry, the output will also be symmetrical wrt. that symmetry. If false, or true but no common symmetry exists, symmetry information is removed from all newly joined SubDs.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Geometry.SubD.JoinSubDs(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Rhino.Geometry.SubD},System.Double,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: MakeDeformable
methods.summary: If possible, converts the object into a form that can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, an non-uniform scaling.

returns.doc: false if object cannot be converted to a deformable object. true if object was already deformable or was converted into a deformable object.

methods.name: MemoryEstimate
methods.summary: Computes an estimate of the number of bytes that this object is using in memory.

returns.doc: An estimated memory footprint.

methods.name: MergeAllCoplanarFaces(Double)
methods.summary: Merges adjacent coplanar faces into single faces.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.

returns.doc: true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

methods.name: MergeAllCoplanarFaces(Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool MergeAllCoplanarFaces(double tolerance, double angleTolerance)
methods.summary: Merges adjacent coplanar faces into single faces.

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Tolerance for determining when edges are adjacent. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAbsoluteTolerance property.

params.name: angleTolerance
params.doc: Angle tolerance, in radians, for determining when faces are parallel. When in doubt, use the document's ModelAngleToleranceRadians property.

returns.doc: true if faces were merged, false if no faces were merged.

methods.name: NonConstOperation
methods.summary: Destroy cache handle

methods.name: Offset
methods.signature: public SubD Offset(double distance, bool solidify)
methods.summary: Makes a new SubD with vertices offset at distance in the direction of the control net vertex normals. Optionally, based on the value of solidify, adds the input SubD and a ribbon of faces along any naked edges.

params.name: distance
params.doc: The distance to offset.

params.name: solidify
params.doc: true if the output SubD should be turned into a closed SubD.

returns.doc: A new SubD if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: OnSwitchToNonConst
methods.summary: Called when this object switches from being considered "owned by the document" to being an independent instance.

methods.name: PackFaces
methods.summary: Resets the SubD to the default face packing if adding creases or deleting faces breaks the quad grids. It does not change the topology or geometry of the SubD. SubD face packs always stop at creases.

returns.doc: The number of face packs.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.signature: public bool Rotate(double angleRadians, Vector3d rotationAxis, Point3d rotationCenter)
methods.summary: Rotates the object about the specified axis. A positive rotation angle results in a counter-clockwise rotation about the axis (right hand rule).

params.name: angleRadians
params.doc: Angle of rotation in radians.

params.name: rotationAxis
params.doc: Direction of the axis of rotation.

params.name: rotationCenter
params.doc: Point on the axis of rotation.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully rotated.

methods.name: Scale
methods.summary: Scales the object by the specified factor. The scale is centered at the origin.

params.name: scaleFactor
params.doc: The uniform scaling factor.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully scaled.

methods.name: SetUserString
methods.signature: public bool SetUserString(string key, string value)
methods.summary: Attach a user string (key,value combination) to this geometry.

params.name: key
params.doc: id used to retrieve this string.

params.name: value
params.doc: string associated with key.

returns.doc: true on success.

methods.name: SetVertexSurfacePoint
methods.signature: public bool SetVertexSurfacePoint(uint vertexIndex, Point3d surfacePoint)
methods.summary: Set the location of a single vertex surface point. This function is not suitable for setting the locations of multiple vertex surface points that are topologically near to each other.

params.name: vertexIndex
params.doc: Index of the vertex to modify

params.name: surfacePoint
params.doc: New surface point location for that vertex

returns.doc: True if a vertex was modified, false otherwise.

methods.name: Subdivide()
methods.summary: Apply the Catmull-Clark subdivision algorithm and save the results in this SubD.

returns.doc: true on success

methods.name: Subdivide(IEnumerable<Int32>)
methods.summary: Apply the Catmull-Clark subdivision algorithm and save the results in this SubD.

params.name: faceIndices
params.doc: Indices of the faces to subdivide.

returns.doc: true on success

methods.name: Subdivide(Int32)
methods.summary: Apply the Catmull-Clark subdivision algorithm and save the results in this SubD.

params.name: count
params.doc: Number of times to subdivide (must be greater than 0)

returns.doc: true on success

methods.name: SurfaceMeshCacheExists
methods.signature: public bool SurfaceMeshCacheExists(bool bTextureCoordinatesExist, bool bCurvaturesExist, bool bColorsExist)
methods.summary: Checks that a surface mesh evaluation cache exists, and that it has the required options. This cache is used by - SurfacePoint(), - ToNurbsCurve(Boolean), and - CreateFromSubD(SubD, Int32).
methods.remarks: This does not check that the cache is up to date, UpdateSurfaceMeshCache(Boolean).

params.name: bTextureCoordinatesExist
params.doc: If True, the cache must contain texture coordinates information.

params.name: bCurvaturesExist
params.doc: If True, the cache must contain curvature information.

params.name: bColorsExist
params.doc: If True, the cache must contain color information.

returns.doc: True if the cache exists on all face fragments and has the required options.

methods.name: ToBrep()
methods.summary: Create a Brep based on this SubD geometry, based on SubDToBrepOptions.Default options.

returns.doc: A new Brep if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: ToBrep(SubDToBrepOptions)
methods.summary: Create a Brep based on this SubD geometry.

params.name: options
params.doc: The SubD to Brep conversion options. Use SubDToBrepOptions.Default for sensible defaults. Currently, these return unpacked faces and locally-G1 vertices in the output Brep.

returns.doc: A new Brep if successful, or null on failure.

methods.name: ToJSON
methods.summary: Create a JSON string representation of this object

params.name: options
params.doc: [Missing <param name="options"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Runtime.CommonObject.ToJSON(Rhino.FileIO.SerializationOptions)"]

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Runtime.CommonObject.ToJSON(Rhino.FileIO.SerializationOptions)"]

methods.name: Transform
methods.summary: Transforms the geometry. If the input Transform has a SimilarityType of OrientationReversing, you may want to consider flipping the transformed geometry after calling this function when it makes sense. For example, you may want to call Flip() on a Brep after transforming it.

params.name: xform
params.doc: Transformation to apply to geometry.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully transformed.

methods.name: TransformComponents
methods.signature: public uint TransformComponents(IEnumerable<ComponentIndex> components, Transform xform, SubDComponentLocation componentLocation)
methods.summary: Transforms an enumerable of SubD components.
methods.remarks: This method does not clear the evaluation cache.

params.name: components
params.doc: The SubD components to transform.

params.name: xform
params.doc: The transformation to apply.

params.name: componentLocation
params.doc: Select between applying the transform to the control net (faster) or the surface points (slower).

returns.doc: The number of vertex locations that changed.

methods.name: Translate(Vector3d)
methods.summary: Translates the object along the specified vector.

params.name: translationVector
params.doc: A moving vector.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully translated.

methods.name: Translate(Double, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public bool Translate(double x, double y, double z)
methods.summary: Translates the object along the specified vector.

params.name: x
params.doc: The X component.

params.name: y
params.doc: The Y component.

params.name: z
params.doc: The Z component.

returns.doc: true if geometry successfully translated.

methods.name: UpdateAllTagsAndSectorCoefficients
methods.summary: Updates vertex tag, edge tag, and edge coefficient values on the active level. After completing custom editing operations that modify the topology of the SubD control net or changing values of vertex or edge tags, the tag and sector coefficients information on nearby components in the edited areas need to be updated.

returns.doc: Number of vertices and edges that were changed during the update.

methods.name: UpdateSurfaceMeshCache
methods.summary: Updates limit surface information returned by - SurfacePoint(), - ToNurbsCurve(Boolean), and - CreateFromSubD(SubD, Int32). The density of the mesh cache is Default().

params.name: lazyUpdate
params.doc: If false, all information is updated. If true, only missing information is updated. If a relatively small subset of a SubD has been modified and care was taken to mark cached subdivision information as stale, then passing true can substantially improve performance.

returns.doc: The number of elements that were updated.

properties.name: Disposed
properties.summary: Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.

properties.name: Empty
properties.summary: Get a new, empty SubD object.

properties.name: HasBrepForm
properties.summary: Returns true if the Brep.TryConvertBrep function will be successful for this object

properties.name: HasUserData
properties.summary: Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

properties.name: IsDeformable
properties.summary: true if object can be accurately modified with "squishy" transformations like projections, shears, and non-uniform scaling.

properties.name: IsDocumentControlled
properties.summary: If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.

properties.name: IsShallowDuplicate
properties.summary: Is this instance a 'shallow copy'. Shallow copies are no different than regular instances with the exception that they will convert to complete independent 'deep' copies if a non const operation is perform on the instance (something that mutates the internal data)

properties.name: IsSolid
properties.summary: Test SubD to see if the active level is a solid. A "solid" is a closed oriented manifold, or a closed oriented manifold.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Tests an object to see if it is valid.

properties.name: ObjectType
properties.summary: Useful for switch statements that need to differentiate between basic object types like points, curves, surfaces, and so on.

properties.name: UserData
properties.summary: List of custom information that is attached to this class.

properties.name: UserDictionary
properties.summary: Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use shareable set of information.

properties.name: UserStringCount
properties.summary: Gets the amount of user strings.
